title: A placeholder positioning assembly, textiles having placeholders therein and related methods
abstract: A placeholder positioning assembly (100) configured to mark a position of at least one structural feature (610) in a textile (600), the placeholder positioning assembly (100) being configured to operate in cooperation with a textile (600) producing machine (10) and to engage the at least one structural feature (610) in the textile (600) with a placeholder (500), includes a placeholder positioning unit (110) configured to engage the at least one placeholder (500) with the at least one structural feature (610) to thereby mark the location of the at least one structural feature (610).
claims:
Claims Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A placeholder positioning assembly configured to mark a position of at least one structural feature in a textile, the placeholder positioning assembly being configured to operate in cooperation with a textile processing machine and to engage the at least one structural feature in the textile with a placeholder, the assembly comprising: a placeholder positioning unit configured to engage the at least one placeholder with the at least one structural feature to thereby mark the location of the at least one structural feature. 2. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 1, further comprising: a holder configured to engage the at least one structural feature in the textile such that the placeholder positioning unit operates in cooperation with the holder to position the at least one placeholder at the at least one structural feature. 3. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, further comprising: at least one anchor arm configured to position at least one anchor member adjacent the at least one structural feature, wherein the placeholder positioning unit is further configured to engage the at least one anchor and the at least one structural feature with the at least one placeholder. 4. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the holder is a knitting tool, knitting needle, slide needle, holding jack, or sinker, of the textile processing machine 5. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claims 3-4, wherein the at least one anchor arm comprises a first anchor arm and a second anchor arm and the at least one anchor member comprises a first anchor member configured to be positioned by the first anchor arm on a side of the at least one structural feature and a second anchor member configured to be positioned by the second anchor arm on an opposing side of the at least one structural feature. 

Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO 6. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claims 3-4, wherein the placeholder positioning unit comprises a placeholder needle and a needle actuator configured to move the needle, and the at least one placeholder is an elongated placeholder, and the needle actuator is configured to move the placeholder needle to engage the at least one placeholder and to position the at least one placeholder through at least the first anchor member at a first position along the first anchor member. 7. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claims 3-6, wherein the placeholder positioning unit comprises a placeholder needle and a needle actuator configured to move the needle, and the needle actuator is configured to move the placeholder needle to engage the at least one placeholder and to position the at least one placeholder through the first anchor member at a first position along the first anchor member, through the at least one structural feature, and through at least the second anchor member at a first position along the second anchor member. 8. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 7, wherein the needle actuator is further configured to move the placeholder needle to thereby shape the placeholder such that the placeholder extends through the structural feature and is secured by the first and second anchor members. 9. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 7, further comprising a placeholder closing unit configured to connect a first portion of the placeholder to a second portion of the placeholder to thereby form a closed shaped placeholder. 10. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 9, wherein the placeholder closing unit comprises at least one of a thermal device configured to weld the first portion to the second portion of the placeholder, an ultrasonic device configured to bond the first portion to the second portion, a knotting unit configured to form a knot with the first and second portion, an adhering unit configured to adhere the first and second portion, an engaging unit configured to durably unite locking features of the first and second portions, or a twisting/crimping unit configured to twist or crimp the first portion and the second portion. 

Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO 11. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 9, wherein the placeholder closing unit comprises at least one of a thermal device configured to weld the placeholder to one or more of the anchor members, an ultrasonic device configured to bond the placeholder to one or more of the anchor members, a knotting unit configured to form a knot with the placeholder and a one or more of the anchor members, an adhering unit configured to adhere the placeholder to one or more of the anchor members, an engaging unit configured to durably unite locking features of either of the placeholder and one or more of the anchor members, or a twisting/crimping unit configured to twist or crimp the portion of the placeholder with one or more of the anchor members. 12. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 9, wherein the placeholder closing unit comprises at least one of a thermal device configured to fix the placeholder by welding a first portion of one or more of the anchor members to a second portion of one or more of the anchor members, an ultrasonic device configured to bond a first portion to a second portion, a knotting unit configured to form a knot with a first portion and a second portion, an adhering unit configured to adhere a first portion to a second portion, an engaging unit configured to durably unite locking features of a first portion to a second portion, or a twisting/crimping unit configured to twist or crimp a first portion and a second portion. 13. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claims 4-12, wherein one or more of the first and second anchor members comprise first and second elongated panels. 14. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 4-13, wherein one or more of the first and second anchor members comprise apertures. 15. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 4-14, wherein one or more of the first and second anchor members comprises first and second ladder structures, each of the first and second ladder structures having elongated parallel members and rungs extending therebetween. 

Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO 16. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 4-15, wherein one or more of the first and second anchor members are flexible. 17. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the at least one placeholder comprises an elastomeric material, a thermoplastic material, a multifilament yarn, a braid, a woven tape, a crochet- or warp-knitted tape, a weft-knitted tape, a tubular knitted cord, a flat plastic tape, a chain of rigid elements, a monofilament yarn, a twisted yarn, an embossed sheet, a flexural non-plastic sheet or tape or combinations thereof. 18. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claims 4-17, wherein the at least one anchor member comprises an elastomeric material, a thermoplastic material, a multifilament yarn, a braid, a woven tape, a crochet- or warp-knitted tape, a weft-knitted tape, a tubular knitted cord, a flat plastic tape, a chain of rigid elements, a monofilament yarn, a twisted yarn, an embossed sheet, a flexural non-plastic sheet or tape or combinations thereof. 19. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 4-18, wherein the first and second anchor arms each comprise at least a pair of elongated members configured to position the respective first and second anchor members in an orientation such that a major surface thereof faces the structural feature. 20. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, further comprising a placeholder dispenser configured to dispense the at least one placeholder to thereby form or shape the at least one placeholder when the placeholder positioning unit engages with the placeholder. 21. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the at least one placeholder comprises a plurality of placeholders, the at least one structural feature comprises a plurality of structural features, and the placeholder positioning unit is configured to position each of the plurality of placeholders through a corresponding structural feature in the plurality of structural features and to engage each of the plurality of placeholders with the at least one anchor member such that each of the plurality of placeholders extends between and the at 

Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO least one anchor member and the corresponding structural feature to thereby mark the position of the plurality of structural features. 22. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claims 2-21, wherein the holder is configured to move the structural feature away from the textile and to enlarge the structural feature. 23. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claims 2-22, wherein the holder is configured to place the structural feature on a needle after placement of the placeholder. 24. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the placeholder positioning assembly is mounted on the textile processing machine. 25. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the placeholder positioning assembly is configured to be removably positioned adjacent the textile processing machine. 26. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the textile processing machine comprising a flat weft knitting machine, a flat warp knitting machine, a crochet machine, a circular weft knitting machine, a circular warp knitting machine, a straight bar knitting machine, or a cotton frame. 27. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the structural feature of the textile comprises a topological feature of the textile that is configured to hold a placeholder on or in the topological feature. 28. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 27, wherein the topological feature comprises a loop or tangle or intersecting points between yarns in the textile. 29. The placeholder positioning assembly of Claim 28, wherein the loop comprises needle loops or sinker loops. 

Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO 30. The placeholder positioning assembly of any preceding claim, wherein the textile comprises a textile panel. 31. A textile comprising a plurality of structural features that are marked for positioning on a second machine, the textile comprising: at least one anchor member; and a plurality of placeholders configured to engage corresponding ones of the plurality of structural features and to connect to the anchor member to thereby mark the plurality of structural features. 32. The textile of Claim 31, where the second machine is a linking machine. 33. The textile of Claim 32, wherein the at least one anchor member comprises a plurality of anchor members comprising at least a first and second anchor member, and the plurality of placeholders are configured to pass through corresponding ones of the plurality of structural features and to extend to and pass through each of the first and second anchor members. 34. The textile of Claims 31-33, wherein the textile comprises a flat weft knit textile, a flat warp knit textile, a crochet textile, a circular weft knit textile, or a circular warp knit textile. 35. The textile of Claims 31-34, wherein the plurality of structural features of the textile each comprise a topological feature of the textile that is configured to hold a placeholder on or in the topological feature. 36. The textile of Claims 31-35, wherein the topologic feature comprises a loop or tangle. 37. The textile of Claims 31-36, wherein the loop comprises needle loops or sinker loops. 

Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO 38. The textile of Claims 31-37, wherein the first and second anchor members comprise first and second elongated panels. 39. The textile of Claims 31-38, wherein the first and second anchor members comprise apertures. 40. The textile of Claims 31-39, wherein the first and second anchor members comprises first and second ladder structures, each of the first and second ladder structures having elongated parallel members and rungs extending therebetween. 41. The textile of Claims 31-40, wherein the first and second anchor members are flexible. 42. The textile of Claims 31-41, wherein the plurality of placeholders comprise an elastomeric material, a thermoplastic material, a multifilament yarn, a braid, a woven tape, a crochet- or warp-knitted tape, a weft-knitted tape, a tubular knitted cord, a flat plastic tape, a chain of rigid elements, a monofilament yarn, a twisted yarn, an embossed sheet, a flexural non- plastic sheet or tape or combinations thereof. 43. A method of saving/marking a plurality of structural features in a textile panel, the method comprising: a) engaging a structural feature of the plurality of structural features in the textile panel; and b) positioning a placeholder of a plurality of placeholders through the structural feature. 44. The method Claim 43, further comprising: c) positioning at least one anchor member adjacent the structural feature; and d) engaging the placeholder with the at least one anchor member such that the placeholder extends between the structural feature and the anchor member to thereby mark the position of the structural feature. 

Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO 45. The method of Claim 44, wherein the at least one anchor member comprises a first and a second anchor member, and step c) comprises positioning the first and second anchor members on opposing sides of the structural feature. 46. The method of Claims 43-45, wherein the placeholder comprises an elongated placeholder, and the step of b) positioning the placeholder comprises engaging the placeholder with a needle and passing the placeholder through the structural feature; or wherein the placeholder comprises a rivet and the step of b) positioning the placeholder comprises inserting the rivet through the structural feature; or wherein the placeholder comprises a loop shape and the step of b) positioning the placeholder comprises passing the loop shape around the structural feature. 47. The method of Claim 46, wherein the step of d) engaging the placeholder with the at least one anchor member comprises using the needle to pass the placeholder through the first and second anchor members. 48. The method of Claim 44, wherein the step of a) engaging a structural feature comprises engaging the structural feature with a holder to move the structural feature in a direction away from the textile panel and enlarging the structural feature. 49. The method of Claim 44, further comprising repeating steps a)-d) with the plurality of structural features such that the plurality of placeholders extend between respective positions along the first and second anchor members and the corresponding structural features of the plurality of structural features to thereby mark the position of the plurality of structural features with the plurality of placeholders. 50. The method of Claims 44-49, wherein the structural feature comprises a loop and step a) comprising removing a loop from a knitting tool of one needle bed, and temporarily holding it on the holder thereby creating a gap between the panel and the needle bed, so that the 

Attorney Docket No.1701.2.WO anchor arm may advance to the other side of the knitting tool, keeping the anchor topologically under the loop. 51. The method of Claims 44-49, wherein the structural feature comprises a loop and step a) comprising transferring the loop from a knitting tool of one needle bed, to a knitting tool of a needle bed substantially on the other side of the panel, for the purpose of creating a gap between the panel and the needle bed, so that the anchor arm may move to the other side of the knitting tool, keeping the anchor topologically under the loop.
